ddls:
CREATE TABLE "media_assets" (
  "asset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each media asset */,
  "tmdb_id" INTEGER /* ID from The Movie Database (TMDb) */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the media asset */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of release */,
  "genres" TEXT /* Genres of the media asset (e.g., Animation, Comedy, Family) */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the media asset */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the media asset in minutes */,
  "vote_count" INTEGER /* Total number of votes received */,
  "vote_avg" REAL /* Average vote rating */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* ID of the director of the media asset */,
  "asset_type" TEXT /* Type of the media asset (e.g., movie, TV show) */,
  "production_company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the production company */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* Country of origin */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the media asset */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the media asset */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the media asset */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the media asset (e.g., in production, released, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("asset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_media_assets_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_media_assets_production_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("production_company_id") REFERENCES production_companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_media_assets_country_of_origin FOREIGN KEY ("country_of_origin") REFERENCES countries ("country_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Full name of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the director */,
  "notable_works" TEXT /* Notable works of the director */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Notable awards received by the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "parent_genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the language */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 639-1 code for the language */,
  "native_name" TEXT /* Name of the language in its native script */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the actor */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the actor */,
  "notable_works" TEXT /* Notable works of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the actor */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "awards" TEXT /* Notable awards received by the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "keywords" (
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each keyword */,
  "keyword_text" TEXT /* Text of the keyword */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the keyword */,
  "popularity" INTEGER /* Popularity of the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "asset_genres" (
  "asset_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each asset-genre association */,
  "asset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media asset */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("asset_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_genres_asset_id FOREIGN KEY ("asset_id") REFERENCES media_assets ("asset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "asset_actors" (
  "asset_actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each asset-actor association */,
  "asset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media asset */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character played by the actor */,
  "role_type" TEXT /* Type of role (e.g., leading, supporting) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("asset_actor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_actors_asset_id FOREIGN KEY ("asset_id") REFERENCES media_assets ("asset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_actors_actor_id FOREIGN KEY ("actor_id") REFERENCES actors ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "asset_keywords" (
  "asset_keyword_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each asset-keyword association */,
  "asset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the media asset */,
  "keyword_id" INTEGER /* ID of the keyword */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("asset_keyword_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_keywords_asset_id FOREIGN KEY ("asset_id") REFERENCES media_assets ("asset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_asset_keywords_keyword_id FOREIGN KEY ("keyword_id") REFERENCES keywords ("keyword_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., content manager, data analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "founded_date" TEXT /* Date the company was founded */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Headquarters location of the company */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code for the country */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
